pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh:7, export_result.sh:6, setup_opensis.sh:146, and direct_db_setup.sh:9. All scripts consistently use this database user.
Verified in setup_task.sh:8, export_result.sh:7, setup_opensis.sh:146, and direct_db_setup.sh:10. All setup scripts consistently configure this password.
Verified in setup_task.sh:9, export_result.sh:8, setup_opensis.sh:145, and direct_db_setup.sh:8. All scripts create and reference database named 'opensis'.
Task description requires adding these two subject areas. Export result script checks for these exact titles in export_result.sh:23-26. Verifier checks for these same titles in verifier.py:45-68.

OpenSIS database schema retrieved from raw.githubusercontent.com/OS4ED/openSIS-Classic/master/install/OpensisSchemaMysqlInc.sql. Table stores subject areas with columns: syear, school_id, subject_id (PK), title, short_name, rollover_id, last_updated, updated_by.
Verified in setup_opensis.sh:171 and direct_db_setup.sh:127. Admin user is created with this username.
Verified in setup_opensis.sh:157-158 and direct_db_setup.sh:65. Setup scripts reference Admin@123 as the admin password.
Verified in direct_db_setup.sh:76. Database setup script inserts version '9.2' into app table.

privileged_info_summary: The task configures OpenSIS Student Information System (v9.2) to add two subject areas: 'Computer Science' and 'Fine Arts'. The system uses MySQL database with credentials: user 'opensis_user', password 'opensis_password_123', database 'opensis'. Subject areas are stored in the 'course_subjects' table with auto-incrementing subject_id as primary key. The OpenSIS application requires admin login with username 'admin' and password 'Admin@123'. All task files consistently reference these credentials and target values. The underlying data is synthetic, generated during environment setup via SQL scripts.
